items.title: Real-estate price estimation
items.description: Platforms predict listing prices from property attributes using linear or regularized linear models as baselines and interpretable comps.

items.title: Demand and sales forecasting
items.description: Retail chains regress weekly units sold on price, promotions, and seasonality indicators when stakeholders need numeric forecasts.

items.title: Energy load prediction
items.description: Utilities forecast megawatt demand from weather and calendar features — continuous y suits linear regression before nonlinear refinements.

items.title: Salary and compensation modeling
items.description: HR analytics regress pay on role, tenure, and location for benchmarking; coefficients summarize marginal associations (not automatic causality).

items.title: A/B test lift estimation
items.description: Experiment analysis often uses linear models on continuous outcomes (revenue per user, session length) with treatment indicator features.

items.title: Scientific trend fitting
items.description: Labs fit calibration lines and dose–response slopes where a linear approximation holds over the measured range.
